sql: SELECT T4.FirstName, T4.LastName, COUNT(T1.TrackId) AS RockTracksPurchased FROM Track AS T1 JOIN Genre AS T2 ON T1.GenreId = T2.GenreId JOIN InvoiceLine AS T3 ON T1.TrackId = T3.TrackId JOIN Invoice AS T5 ON T3.InvoiceId = T5.InvoiceId JOIN Customer AS T4 ON T5.CustomerId = T4.CustomerId WHERE T2.Name = 'Rock' GROUP BY T4.CustomerId ORDER BY RockTracksPurchased DESC LIMIT 1;

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN candidate AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Weight > (SELECT AVG(Weight) FROM people WHERE Sex = 'M');

sql: SELECT T1.policy_type_code, T2.AverageSettlement FROM Available_Policies AS T1 JOIN (SELECT T3.Policy_ID, AVG(T5.Settlement_Amount) as AverageSettlement FROM First_Notification_of_Loss AS T3 JOIN Claims AS T4 ON T3.FNOL_ID = T4.FNOL_ID JOIN Settlements AS T5 ON T4.Claim_ID = T5.Claim_ID GROUP BY T3.Policy_ID) AS T2 ON T1.Policy_ID = T2.Policy_ID;

sql: SELECT T3.course_name, T1.registration_date, T4.first_name, T4.last_name FROM Student_Course_Registrations AS T1 JOIN (SELECT course_id, MAX(registration_date) AS max_reg_date FROM Student_Course_Registrations GROUP BY course_id) AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id AND T1.registration_date = T2.max_reg_date JOIN Courses AS T3 ON T1.course_id = T3.course_id JOIN People AS T4 ON T1.student_id = T4.person_id;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows identifying the last student to register for each course, listing the course name, the final registration date, and the student's first and last name.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_name, SUM(T4.product_price) AS total_spent_on_completed_orders FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T2.order_status_code = 'Completed' GROUP BY T1.customer_name;

sql: SELECT Country, GenreName, TracksSold FROM (SELECT T5.BillingCountry AS Country, T2.Name AS GenreName, COUNT(T1.TrackId) AS TracksSold, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY T5.BillingCountry ORDER BY COUNT(T1.TrackId) DESC) as rn FROM Track AS T1 JOIN Genre AS T2 ON T1.GenreId = T2.GenreId JOIN InvoiceLine AS T3 ON T1.TrackId = T3.TrackId JOIN Invoice AS T5 ON T3.InvoiceId = T5.InvoiceId GROUP BY T5.BillingCountry, T2.Name) AS T6 WHERE T6.rn = 1;
result_summary: Returns 24 rows identifying the most popular music genre within each country, based on the total number of tracks sold.

sql: SELECT Location, SUM(Capacity) AS Total_Capacity FROM cinema GROUP BY Location ORDER BY Total_Capacity DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Returns 1 row identifying the single location with the highest total cinema capacity, calculated by summing the capacities of all cinemas within that location.
schema:

sql: SELECT T3.Airline, COUNT(*) / 2 AS RoundTrips FROM flights AS T1 JOIN flights AS T2 ON T1.Airline = T2.Airline AND TRIM(T1.SourceAirport) = TRIM(T2.DestAirport) AND TRIM(T1.DestAirport) = TRIM(T2.SourceAirport) JOIN airlines AS T3 ON T1.Airline = T3.uid WHERE TRIM(T1.SourceAirport) < TRIM(T1.DestAirport) GROUP BY T3.Airline;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing each airline and the total count of its round-trip routes, where a route has service between two airports in both directions.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, SUM(T3.cost_of_treatment) AS total_cost FROM Owners AS T1 JOIN Dogs AS T2 ON T1.owner_id = T2.owner_id JOIN Treatments AS T3 ON T2.dog_id = T3.dog_id WHERE T2.abandoned_yn = '0' GROUP BY T1.owner_id HAVING SUM(T3.cost_of_treatment) > 500;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows identifying owners of non-abandoned dogs whose total treatment costs exceeded 500, showing each owner's full name and their total calculated cost.

sql: SELECT T1.product_type_code, AVG(T1.daily_hire_cost) AS avg_cost, COUNT(T2.booking_id) AS booking_count FROM Products_for_Hire AS T1 JOIN Products_Booked AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id GROUP BY T1.product_type_code HAVING AVG(T1.daily_hire_cost) > 20;
result_summary: Returns 1 row showing the product type, average daily hire cost, and total booking count for product categories where the average hire cost is greater than $20.

sql: SELECT T1.Debate_ID, T4.Venue FROM debate_people AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.Affirmative = T2.People_ID JOIN people AS T3 ON T1.Negative = T3.People_ID JOIN debate AS T4 ON T1.Debate_ID = T4.Debate_ID WHERE T2.Party = 'Republican' AND T3.Party = 'Republican';

sql: SELECT T1.Event_ID, COUNT(T2.Participant_ID) FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Participants_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID GROUP BY T1.Event_ID;
result_summary: Returns 12 rows listing each event ID along with the total count of participants registered for that event.

sql: SELECT Participant_Details FROM Participants WHERE Participant_ID NOT IN (SELECT T2.Participant_ID FROM Events AS T1 JOIN Participants_in_Events AS T2 ON T1.Event_ID = T2.Event_ID WHERE T1.Event_Details = 'Fail');
